 {"id":"bd-26y68","title":"Implement health classification engine","description":"Implement health classification engine for pane status.

## Status Categories

| Status | Detection Criteria |
|--------|-------------------|
| healthy | Agent process running, recent output (<5min), no error patterns |
| rate_limited | Output contains rate limit patterns (from bd-1mcvs) |
| unresponsive | No output for >5min AND process still running |
| crashed | No agent process OR zombie state |
| unknown | Cannot determine state |

## Implementation

Reuse status enrichments from bd-1g9wv:
- rate_limit_detected
- last_output_ts / seconds_since_output
- process_state
- child_pid

Aggregate into single classification per pane.

## Configuration

- UNRESPONSIVE_THRESHOLD_SECONDS: 300 (5 min)
- Allow override via flag or config

## Edge Cases

- New pane (no history): treat as healthy if process running
- Agent in long operation: distinguish from stuck","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T23:27:22.326210325Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-20T06:27:48.663555396Z","closed_at":"2026-01-20T06:27:48.663373874Z","close_reason":"Implemented as part of bd-31e1f diagnose.go - includes --diagnose-pane flag, overall health classification, --diagnose-fix auto-remediation, recommendation generator, and health classification engine","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-26y68","depends_on_id":"bd-31e1f","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T23:34:26Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"bd-27gv","title":"Add --no-tui flag for scripting and CI environments","description":"## Problem
Dashboard may not work in headless/CI environments.

## Code Review Findings (2026-01-19)

### Partial Support Exists

Found terminal detection in multiple files:
- output/format.go:132-133 - `IsTerminal()` function
- output/success.go:71 - `isStdoutTerminal()`
- output/errors.go:52 - `isStderrTerminal()`
- cli/util.go:38 - Uses isatty library

### Missing: --no-tui Flag

No `--no-tui` or `--json` flags found for the dashboard command.

## Fix Implementation

### 1. Add Flags to Dashboard Command
```go
// cmd/ntm/dashboard.go
var dashboardCmd = &cobra.Command{
    Use:   "dashboard",
    Short: "Interactive dashboard",
    RunE: func(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) error {
        noTUI, _ := cmd.Flags().GetBool("no-tui")
        jsonOut, _ := cmd.Flags().GetBool("json")

        // Auto-detect non-interactive
        if !output.IsTerminal() || os.Getenv("CI") != "" {
            noTUI = true
        }

        if jsonOut {
            return runDashboardJSON()
        }
        if noTUI {
            return runDashboardPlain()
        }
        return runDashboardTUI()
    },
}

func init() {
    dashboardCmd.Flags().Bool("no-tui", false, "Plain text output (no interactive UI)")
    dashboardCmd.Flags().Bool("json", false, "JSON output (implies --no-tui)")
}
```

### 2. Plain Mode Output
```go
func runDashboardPlain() error {
    status := getDashboardStatus()

    fmt.Printf("Fleet: %d/%d agents active\n", status.Active, status.Total)
    fmt.Printf("  Claude: %d  Codex: %d  Gemini: %d\n",
        status.Claude, status.Codex, status.Gemini)

    if status.Alerts > 0 {
        fmt.Printf("\nAlerts: %d critical, %d warning\n",
            status.Critical, status.Warning)
    }

    fmt.Printf("\nBeads: %d ready, %d in-progress\n",
        status.ReadyBeads, status.InProgressBeads)

    return nil
}
```

### 3. JSON Mode Output
```go
func runDashboardJSON() error {
    status := getDashboardStatus()
    return json.NewEncoder(os.Stdout).Encode(status)
}
```

## Unit Tests Required
```go
func TestDashboardNoTUIFlag(t *testing.T) {
    cmd := rootCmd
    cmd.SetArgs([]string{"dashboard", "--no-tui"})

    var out bytes.Buffer
    cmd.SetOut(&out)
    cmd.Execute()

    output := out.String()
    if strings.Contains(output, "\x1b[") {
        t.Error("--no-tui should not contain ANSI codes")
    }
    if !strings.Contains(output, "Fleet:") {
        t.Error("--no-tui should contain fleet status")
    }
}

func TestDashboardJSONFlag(t *testing.T) {
    cmd := rootCmd
    cmd.SetArgs([]string{"dashboard", "--json"})

    var out bytes.Buffer
    cmd.SetOut(&out)
    cmd.Execute()

    var status map[string]interface{}
    if err := json.Unmarshal(out.Bytes(), &status); err != nil {
        t.Errorf("--json output is not valid JSON: %v", err)
    }
}

func TestDashboardCIAutoDetect(t *testing.T) {
    t.Setenv("CI", "true")

    // Should auto-select plain mode in CI
    cmd := rootCmd
    cmd.SetArgs([]string{"dashboard"})

    var out bytes.Buffer
    cmd.SetOut(&out)
    cmd.Execute()

    output := out.String()
    if strings.Contains(output, "\x1b[") {
        t.Error("CI environment should auto-use plain mode")
    }
}
```

## E2E Test
```bash
# Test --no-tui
ntm dashboard --no-tui | grep -q "Fleet:" && echo "PASS" || echo "FAIL"

# Test --json
ntm dashboard --json | jq .fleet.total && echo "PASS" || echo "FAIL"

# Test CI mode
CI=true ntm dashboard 2>&1 | grep -q "Fleet:" && echo "PASS" || echo "FAIL"

# Test piping
ntm dashboard --no-tui | head -1 | grep -q "Fleet:" && echo "PASS" || echo "FAIL"
```

## Verification
- [ ] --no-tui flag outputs plain text
- [ ] --json flag outputs valid JSON
- [ ] CI=1 auto-detects plain mode
- [ ] Piped output works: ntm dashboard --no-tui | grep","notes":"Implemented --no-tui, --json, and --debug flags for dashboard command. Added shouldUsePlainMode() for auto-detecting CI environments, isTUIDebugEnabled() for NTM_TUI_DEBUG support. Plain text and JSON output modes now fully functional.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T07:13:49.112776980Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-20T06:08:33.816282755Z","closed_at":"2026-01-20T06:08:29.329890849Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-27gv","depends_on_id":"bd-2g6y","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T23:34:26Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-27gv","depends_on_id":"bd-36he","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T23:34:26Z","created_by":"import"}]}
+{"id":"bd-27hn0","title":"EPIC: Privacy & Secrets Safety (Redaction + Privacy Mode)","description":"## Summary
NTM orchestrates many AI agents and therefore routinely handles **high-risk text**: API keys, OAuth tokens, credentials, secrets in env/config, and sometimes PII in logs/prompt history/pane scrollback. Today, individual features occasionally mention redaction (e.g. checkpoint export), but NTM lacks a **single, consistent, centrally-tested redaction + privacy policy layer**.

This epic adds a unified **Secrets/PII safety pipeline** and a first-class **Privacy Mode** so users can confidently use NTM in sensitive repos without accidental leakage via:
- `ntm send` / palette prompts
- `ntm copy` / `ntm save`
- checkpoints / exports
- Agent Mail messages
- REST/WS/web dashboards (as they mature)
- event logs, prompt history, archives

## Goals
- Provide one canonical Go implementation for **detection + redaction** with strong unit tests.
- Make safe behavior easy: sensible defaults + ergonomic flags and config.
- Ensure **no silent data loss**: redact intentionally; when blocking, explain clearly.
- Ensure robot outputs remain stable and machine-readable.

## Non-goals
- A full enterprise DLP product.
- Perfect secret detection (we want high-signal, low false positives; allow overrides).

## Proposed Design (high level)
- Introduce a small internal package (name TBD, e.g. `internal/safety/redaction`) that:
  - detects sensitive strings (provider keys, JWTs, common secret patterns)
  - supports deterministic redaction (stable placeholders)
  - supports modes: `off | warn | redact | block`
  - supports allowlisting (explicit user override / per-project patterns)
- Integrate the pipeline at all high-risk IO boundaries (see child features/tasks).

## Success Criteria
- A user can enable privacy/redaction via config or flags and be confident that:
  - clipboard/file outputs and logs do not leak raw secrets
  - automated exports (support bundles/checkpoints) are safe-by-default
  - errors explain what was blocked and how to override safely
- Comprehensive tests exist (unit + E2E) with clear structured logging.

## Notes
- Prefer standard library crypto + regex; avoid new deps unless unavoidable.
- Keep implementation incremental: land library + one integration point, then expand.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:19:44.323969164Z","created_by":"OrangeWolf","updated_at":"2026-02-01T03:19:44.323969164Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-27l8q","title":"rano: Implement PID-to-pane mapping for agent attribution","description":"Create mechanism to map process PIDs to NTM pane identities.

## Challenge

rano sees PIDs, NTM sees panes. Need to bridge:
- Pane "agent-1" has shell PID 1234
- Agent process (claude, codex) is child of shell
- May have multiple child processes (node, etc.)

## Implementation

### 1. Get Shell PID per Pane
```go
func GetPanePID(session, pane string) (int, error) {
    // tmux list-panes -t session:pane -F '#{pane_pid}'
    cmd := exec.Command("tmux", "list-panes", "-t", fmt.Sprintf("%s:%s", session, pane), "-F", "#{pane_pid}")
    output, err := cmd.Output()
    // Parse PID
}
```

### 2. Get Child Processes
```go
func GetChildPIDs(parentPID int) ([]int, error) {
    // Read /proc/<pid>/task/<tid>/children
    // Or use pgrep -P <pid>
}
```

### 3. Build PID Map
```go
type PIDMap struct {
    paneToMainPID   map[string]int      // pane -> shell PID
    pidToPane       map[int]string      // any PID -> pane
    mu              sync.RWMutex
}

func (m *PIDMap) Refresh() {
    for pane := range m.paneToMainPID {
        shellPID := getShellPID(pane)
        childPIDs := getChildPIDs(shellPID)
        // Update maps
    }
}
```

## Code Location
- internal/integrations/rano/pidmap.go

## Testing
- Spawn pane, verify PID mapping works
- Verify child process discovery","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-21T07:14:39.397643370Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-22T01:16:22.448544109Z","closed_at":"2026-01-22T01:16:22.448465561Z","close_reason":"Implemented PID-to-pane mapping in internal/integrations/rano/pidmap.go with comprehensive unit tests. Provides PIDMap struct with refresh logic, child process discovery via /proc, and bidirectional PID-to-pane lookups for rano network attribution.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-27l8q","depends_on_id":"bd-308f5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T23:34:26Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-27l8q","depends_on_id":"bd-33wzo","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T23:34:26Z","created_by":"import"}]}

## Design

```
┌─ Network Activity ──────────────────────────────┐

│ ────────────────────────────────────────────────│
│ agent-1      3    45KB   120KB    250ms  ▲▲▲    │
│ agent-2      1    10KB    50KB    180ms  ▲      │
│ agent-3      0     0KB     0KB      -    (idle) │
│ ────────────────────────────────────────────────│
│ Total        4    55KB   170KB                  │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

- Real-time connection count
- Bytes sent/received (human-readable)
- Latency indicators
- Activity sparkline or indicators
- Provider breakdown on expand

 {"id":"bd-3cdsi","title":"Integration: Add swarm state to --robot-snapshot output","description":"## Purpose

Extend `--robot-snapshot` output to include active swarm orchestration state, providing a unified view of the system including swarm allocations, session health, and agent distribution.

## IMPORTANT: API Design Consistency

This integrates with the EXISTING `--robot-snapshot` command. We do NOT create a separate `--robot-swarm` flag.

```bash
# Existing command, now with swarm data
ntm --robot-snapshot
```

## Extended Output Schema

```json
{
  "timestamp": "2026-01-22T10:30:00Z",
  "sessions": [...],
  "beads": {...},
  "alerts": [...],
  "mail": {...},
  
  "swarm": {
    "active": true,
    "plan": {
      "created_at": "2026-01-22T09:00:00Z",
      "scan_dir": "/projects",
      "total_agents": 24,
      "allocations": [
        {
          "project": "ntm",
          "tier": 1,
          "open_beads": 450,
          "cc_agents": 3,
          "cod_agents": 2,
          "gmi_agents": 1
        }
      ]
    },
    "sessions": [
      {
        "name": "cc_agents_1",
        "agent_type": "cc",
        "pane_count": 8,
        "healthy": 7,
        "limit_hit": 1
      }
    ],
    "health": {
      "total_agents": 24,
      "healthy": 22,
      "limit_hit": 1,
      "respawning": 1
    },
    "recent_events": [
      {
        "type": "limit_hit",
        "pane": "cc_agents_1:1.3",
        "timestamp": "2026-01-22T10:25:00Z"
      }
    ]
  }
}
```

## Implementation

```go
// In internal/robot/snapshot.go

func PrintSnapshot(cfg *config.Config) error {
    snapshot := buildBaseSnapshot()
    
    // Add swarm state if active
    if swarmState := getActiveSwarmState(); swarmState != nil {
        snapshot.Swarm = &SwarmSnapshot{
            Active:       true,
            Plan:         swarmState.Plan,
            Sessions:     buildSessionsSummary(swarmState),
            Health:       buildHealthSummary(swarmState),
            RecentEvents: swarmState.RecentEvents(10),
        }
    }
    
    return outputJSON(snapshot)
}
```

## Conditional Inclusion

- `swarm` key is ONLY present if a swarm is active
- If no swarm running, the key is omitted (not null)
- Keeps output minimal when swarm feature not in use

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Swarm state included in --robot-snapshot when active
- [ ] Swarm key omitted when no swarm running
- [ ] Plan allocations included
- [ ] Per-session health summary
- [ ] Recent events (limit hits, respawns)
- [ ] Aggregate health counts","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-22T07:02:36.342076125Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-01T02:24:59.573077374Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3cdsi","depends_on_id":"bd-3lo54","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T23:34:28Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-3cdsi","depends_on_id":"bd-3uda9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T23:34:28Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3cewt","title":"Test: Unit tests for AllocationCalculator","description":"## Purpose
Comprehensive unit tests for BeadScanner with mocked br CLI to verify project scanning and bead counting behavior.

## Test Structure

```go
// internal/swarm/scanner_test.go
package swarm

import (
    "os"
    "os/exec"
    "path/filepath"
    "testing"
)

// MockBrCommand simulates br CLI responses
func TestBeadScanner_Scan(t *testing.T) {
    // Create temp directory structure
    tmpDir := t.TempDir()
    t.Logf("Test directory: %s", tmpDir)
    
    // Create mock project directories
    projects := []string{"project-a", "project-b", "no-beads-project"}
    for _, p := range projects {
        dir := filepath.Join(tmpDir, p)
        require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(dir, 0755))
        t.Logf("Created project dir: %s", dir)
    }
    
    // Create .beads directories for projects with beads
    createMockBeadsDir(t, filepath.Join(tmpDir, "project-a"), 15)
    createMockBeadsDir(t, filepath.Join(tmpDir, "project-b"), 5)
    
    t.Logf("Mock .beads created - project-a: 15 beads, project-b: 5 beads")
    
    // Create scanner with mock executor
    mockExec := &MockBrExecutor{
        responses: map[string]string{
            filepath.Join(tmpDir, "project-a"): `[{"id":"bd-001","status":"open"},{"id":"bd-002","status":"open"}]`,
            filepath.Join(tmpDir, "project-b"): `[{"id":"bd-003","status":"open"}]`,
        },
    }
    
    scanner := NewBeadScanner(testLogger(t))
    scanner.executor = mockExec
    
    // Execute scan
    results, err := scanner.Scan(tmpDir)
    require.NoError(t, err, "Scan should succeed")
    
    t.Logf("Scan results: %d projects found", len(results))
    for _, r := range results {
        t.Logf("  %s: %d open beads", r.Name, r.OpenBeads)
    }
    
    // Verify results
    assert.Len(t, results, 2, "Should find 2 projects with beads")
    
    // Verify project-a
    projA := findProject(results, "project-a")
    require.NotNil(t, projA, "project-a should be found")
    assert.Equal(t, 2, projA.OpenBeads, "project-a should have 2 open beads")
    
    // Verify project without beads is excluded
    projNone := findProject(results, "no-beads-project")
    assert.Nil(t, projNone, "no-beads-project should be excluded")
}

func TestBeadScanner_NoBrInstalled(t *testing.T) {
    scanner := NewBeadScanner(testLogger(t))
    scanner.brPath = "/nonexistent/br"
    
    t.Log("Testing with non-existent br binary")
    
    tmpDir := t.TempDir()
    createMockBeadsDir(t, tmpDir, 5)
    
    results, err := scanner.Scan(tmpDir)
    
    t.Logf("Error returned: %v", err)
    
    // Should gracefully handle missing br
    assert.Error(t, err, "Should return error for missing br")
    assert.Nil(t, results)
}

func TestBeadScanner_InvalidJSON(t *testing.T) {
    mockExec := &MockBrExecutor{
        responses: map[string]string{
            "/test": "invalid json {{{",
        },
    }
    
    scanner := NewBeadScanner(testLogger(t))
    scanner.executor = mockExec
    
    t.Log("Testing with invalid JSON response from br")
    
    _, err := scanner.scanProject("/test")
    
    t.Logf("Error: %v", err)
    assert.Error(t, err, "Should error on invalid JSON")
    assert.Contains(t, err.Error(), "parse", "Error should mention parsing")
}

func TestBeadScanner_EmptyDirectory(t *testing.T) {
    tmpDir := t.TempDir()
    t.Logf("Testing empty directory: %s", tmpDir)
    
    scanner := NewBeadScanner(testLogger(t))
    results, err := scanner.Scan(tmpDir)
    
    require.NoError(t, err, "Empty dir should not error")
    assert.Empty(t, results, "Should return empty results")
    t.Log("Empty directory handled correctly - no projects found")
}

func TestBeadScanner_DeepNesting(t *testing.T) {
    tmpDir := t.TempDir()
    
    // Create deeply nested structure
    deepPath := filepath.Join(tmpDir, "level1", "level2", "level3", "project")
    require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(deepPath, 0755))
    createMockBeadsDir(t, deepPath, 10)
    
    t.Logf("Created deep project at: %s", deepPath)
    
    scanner := NewBeadScanner(testLogger(t))
    scanner.maxDepth = 5
    
    results, err := scanner.Scan(tmpDir)
    require.NoError(t, err)
    
    t.Logf("Found %d projects at depth 4", len(results))
    
    // Should find the deep project
    assert.Len(t, results, 1, "Should find nested project")
}

// Helper: create mock .beads directory
func createMockBeadsDir(t *testing.T, projectPath string, beadCount int) {
    beadsDir := filepath.Join(projectPath, ".beads")
    require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(beadsDir, 0755))
    
    // Create mock issues.jsonl
    issuesPath := filepath.Join(beadsDir, "issues.jsonl")
    f, err := os.Create(issuesPath)
    require.NoError(t, err)
    defer f.Close()
    
    for i := 0; i < beadCount; i++ {
        fmt.Fprintf(f, `{"id":"bd-%d","status":"open"}\n`, i)
    }
    
    t.Logf("Created %s with %d beads", issuesPath, beadCount)
}

// Helper: test logger that outputs to t.Log
func testLogger(t *testing.T) *slog.Logger {
    return slog.New(slog.NewTextHandler(testWriter{t}, &slog.HandlerOptions{
        Level: slog.LevelDebug,
    }))
}

type testWriter struct{ t *testing.T }
func (w testWriter) Write(p []byte) (int, error) {
    w.t.Log(string(p))
    return len(p), nil
}
```

## Logging Requirements
- Log test directory paths
- Log mock data setup details
- Log scan results with counts
- Log errors with context
- Use test logger that outputs to t.Log

- [ ] Test projects without .beads excluded
- [ ] Test invalid JSON handling
- [ ] Test empty directory
- [ ] Test deep nesting with max depth
- [ ] Test missing br binary
- [ ] Comprehensive t.Logf output

+{"id":"bd-3cgwv","title":"Feature: Unified Secrets/PII Redaction Engine","description":"## Summary
Create a single, canonical Go implementation for detecting and redacting sensitive strings (API keys, tokens, credentials, common secret patterns) so NTM can apply it consistently across commands, robot outputs, exports, and integrations.

## Why
NTM’s value prop (multi-agent orchestration) amplifies the blast radius of accidental secret/PII leakage:
- prompts are broadcast to many panes
- outputs are copied/saved/exported
- robot endpoints will be exposed via REST/WS/web

A centralized library prevents each subsystem from rolling its own ad-hoc regexes.

## Requirements
- Deterministic redaction: same input → same placeholder (stable for diffing), but never reversible.
- Configurable modes: `off | warn | redact | block`.
- Allowlist escapes: per-project opt-outs for known-false-positives.
- Low false-positive bias: blocking is expensive; prefer warn/redact unless user opts into block.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Library API is small + unit tested.
- Provides structured results:
  - what was detected (category)
  - what was redacted
  - whether action should be warn/block
- Default patterns cover at least: OpenAI, Anthropic, GitHub tokens, generic JWTs, AWS keys, common `*_API_KEY=` env patterns.

## Follow-ups
- Integrations (send/copy/save/mail/bundles) are separate tasks under this epic.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:20:09.189791935Z","created_by":"OrangeWolf","updated_at":"2026-02-01T03:20:09.201946538Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3cgwv","depends_on_id":"bd-27hn0","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:20:09.201909950Z","created_by":"OrangeWolf"}]}

 {"id":"bd-3cw6i","title":"EPIC: Command Kernel + Parity Gate","description":"## Summary\nBuild the Command Kernel registry to enforce mechanical parity between CLI, TUI, REST, OpenAPI, and Web UI command palette.\n\n## Scope\n- Create internal/kernel registry with command metadata, schemas, and REST bindings.\n- Refactor internal/robot into GetX() + PrintX() to share structs with API.\n- Extract service layer (SessionService, AgentService, OutputService, etc.).\n- Wire CLI/TUI commands through kernel (no ad‑hoc cobra actions).\n- Parity Gate CI: kernel coverage + OpenAPI spec diff + CLI vs API output parity.\n\n## Success Criteria\n- Every CLI command is registered in kernel with REST + OpenAPI metadata.\n- OpenAPI generated from kernel matches checked‑in spec.\n- Parity tests prevent drift.\n","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-23T22:47:59.274688688Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-23T22:57:47.535499508Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3cw6i","depends_on_id":"bd-1nqde","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T23:34:28Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-3cw6i","depends_on_id":"bd-1wsxq","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T23:34:28Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-3cw6i","depends_on_id":"bd-1xc00","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T23:34:28Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-3cw6i","depends_on_id":"bd-vmwdj","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T23:34:28Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-3cw6i","depends_on_id":"bd-yn7qe","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T23:34:28Z","created_by":"import"}]}
